mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals weak labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the coefficient on UNRATE in regression models is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates strong real economic activity, which drives corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and HOUST has a coefficient < 0.05 in regression models, or 20-day returns fail to rise when both series exceed their 3-month moving averages.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending growth (RSAFS) during a late-cycle expansion phase signals sustained demand momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances where RSAFS growth exceeds 1.5% month-over-month, or the correlation between RSAFS and SPX returns falls below 0.2 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening expectations, which raises equity discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or the coefficient on CPIAUCSL in regression models is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates a supportive environment for equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between HOUST and FEDFUNDS has a coefficient < 0.05 in regression models, or 20-day returns fail to rise when both series exceed their 6-month highs.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) during a late-cycle expansion phase signals resilient labor markets that support consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 2.0% month-over-month, or the correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns falls below 0.3 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) during a post-recession recovery phase indicates expanding corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0% quarter-over-quarter, or the coefficient on GDPC1 in regression models is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
